skill.name: shift-handover-summarizer
skill.description: Generate structured shift handover summaries from EHR records, highlighting critical events, vital sign changes, and pending tasks for incoming clinical staff.
skill.summary: Automatically transform EHR records into structured, priority-ranked shift handover summaries for clinical staff.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 57 static analysis findings are false positives. The scanner flagged the word 'description' as a weak cryptographic algorithm and the variable 'key_events' as certificate/key files. Shell command findings reference bash code blocks in markdown documentation, not executed commands. Hardcoded URL findings are GitHub source links. Heuristic dangerous combination findings match nonexistent patterns. The Python script is a well-structured CLI tool with no security vulnerabilities. The only real risk factor is legitimate file read/write operations for processing EHR records.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'description' variable usage as a cryptographic algorithm. The word 'description' is used as a clinical data field name throughout the code (patient record descriptions, CLI argument descriptions). No cryptographic algorithms are present in the code.

low_findings.title: Certificate/key files false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'key_events' variable as certificate/key file access. The variable refers to clinical events that are key to patient care, not cryptographic keys. This is a naming collision with security terminology.

low_findings.title: Shell backtick execution false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick patterns in SKILL.md. These appear inside markdown code blocks showing example CLI usage (python -m py_compile, python scripts/main.py --help). They are documentation, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Heuristic dangerous combination false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer heuristic flagged a combination of code execution, network, and credential access patterns. No actual code execution, network calls, or credential access exists in the code. The heuristic matched on documentation examples and variable naming, not actual behavior.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged data field access on patient records as system reconnaissance. The code calls record.get('patient_id') and similar methods which is normal data extraction from provided JSON records, not system probing.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged GitHub URL in SKILL.md line 7. This is a source repository reference in documentation, not a network call. No network requests are made by the skill.

content.user_title: Generate Shift Handover Summaries from EHR Records
content.value_statement: Healthcare professionals spend significant time compiling shift handover reports from electronic health records. This skill automatically generates structured, priority-ranked summaries highlighting critical events, vital sign changes, and pending tasks for incoming staff.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes structured EHR records and extracts key clinical events within a shift time range
Ranks patients by event severity into high, medium, and low priority categories
Detects abnormal vital signs based on configurable thresholds for heart rate, blood pressure, temperature, and SpO2
Identifies critical events using configurable keyword patterns such as resuscitation and cardiac arrest
Generates pending task suggestions for incoming clinical staff based on patient status
Supports department filtering and configurable output to file or stdout
limitations: Processes only pre-exported structured JSON records, not live hospital systems or EHR APIs
Event classification depends on preset keyword thresholds that may need adjustment per department
Does not replace direct verbal handover or physician sign-off for clinical decisions
Cannot access patient data from external databases, only from files you provide

use_cases.title: End-of-Shift Nursing Handover
use_cases.description: Nurses at shift change can input the shift EHR records to generate a structured summary highlighting patient status changes and pending tasks for the incoming team.

use_cases.title: Resident Morning Round Preparation
use_cases.description: Residents can review overnight events by processing EHR records to identify critical changes and prioritize patients requiring immediate attention during rounds.

use_cases.title: Department Shift Review
use_cases.description: Department heads can generate overview reports with department-filtered summaries of all patients, events, and statistics for quality review and process improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Shift Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a shift handover summary from the records file at data/records.json for the shift starting at 07:00 and ending at 15:00. List patients by priority and include key events and pending tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: A nurse needs a quick end-of-shift handover summary for the incoming team.

prompt_templates.title: Department-Specific Night Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Process records at data/shift_records.json for the night shift from 22:00 to 06:00. Filter to Cardiology department only. Exclude vital signs and save the output to cardiology_night_report.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: A cardiology ward nurse needs a night shift handover focused only on events and medications.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Threshold Handover
prompt_templates.prompt: Using records.json for the 14:00 to 22:00 shift, customize the thresholds: set high heart rate to 130 bpm and low SpO2 to 92 percent. Focus on patients with critical events and list all pending tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: A charge nurse needs a detailed handover using department-specific vital sign thresholds.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shift Trend Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze three consecutive shift records files named morning.json, afternoon.json, and night.json covering a full 24-hour period. Identify patients whose condition deteriorated across shifts and note unresolved pending tasks from prior shifts.
prompt_templates.scenario: A clinical coordinator needs to identify care gaps and deterioration trends across multiple shifts.

output_examples.input: Generate handover for Cardiology shift from 07:00 to 15:00 on 2026-02-06 using records from data/shift_records.json.
output_examples.output: A structured handover report with three sections: high priority patients listed first with bed numbers and critical events, medium priority patients requiring attention with their symptoms, and stable patients grouped together. The report includes shift statistics showing total patients, resuscitation events, and department overview.

output_examples.input: Summarize overnight events for the ICU department from 22:00 to 06:00 using records at icu_night.json. Exclude vitals and save to icu_report.json.
output_examples.output: A text report listing ICU patients ranked by event severity with key events, medication changes, procedures performed, and pending tasks. The summary overview shows total patients, critical patient count, and a plain-text handover narrative for verbal handoff support.

output_examples.input: Review the morning shift records for the Emergency Department. Customize the thresholds: high heart rate 130, low SpO2 88.
output_examples.output: A priority-sorted patient list with department-specific threshold alerts. High priority patients show flagged events like cardiac arrest or respiratory distress. The report includes vital sign summaries at critical time points and suggested monitoring tasks for incoming staff.

best_practices: Always provide explicit timezone offsets in shift start and end times to avoid incorrect event filtering
Verify the generated output against original records for completeness, especially for high-priority events
Configure vital sign threshold values to match department-specific clinical protocols before processing
anti_patterns: Using this skill for real-time patient monitoring or emergency triage decisions
Relying solely on keyword-based event detection without clinical verification by qualified staff
Processing patient data without ensuring compliance with applicable privacy regulations such as HIPAA

faq.question: What data format does this skill require?
faq.answer: Structured JSON records with patient information and timestamped clinical events including vital signs, medications, procedures, and event notes.

faq.question: Can this skill access live hospital systems or EHR APIs?
faq.answer: No. It only processes pre-exported JSON files that you provide. It does not connect to any external systems or databases.

faq.question: How are patients prioritized?
faq.answer: Based on event severity determined by preset keywords such as resuscitation and cardiac arrest, plus configurable vital sign thresholds for heart rate, blood pressure, temperature, and SpO2.

faq.question: Can I customize the vital sign thresholds?
faq.answer: Yes. Default thresholds include heart rate above 120 or below 50 bpm, systolic BP above 180 or below 90, temperature above 38.5 C, and SpO2 below 90 percent. All thresholds are configurable.

faq.question: Is this skill HIPAA compliant?
faq.answer: This skill processes data locally on your machine. You must ensure your data handling and storage practices comply with applicable privacy regulations.

faq.question: Does this skill replace verbal handover or physician sign-off?
faq.answer: No. It is a documentation aid only. Direct verbal handover between clinical staff and physician sign-off are still required for safe patient care.
